ddls:
CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Parent category ID (for sub-categories) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the channel */,
  "channel_description" TEXT /* Description of the channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie the actor is in */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "actor_description" TEXT /* Description of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie the director directed */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "director_description" TEXT /* Description of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being rated */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating of the movie */,
  "rating_source" TEXT /* Source of the rating (e.g., IMDB, Rotten Tomatoes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "watch_history" (
  "watch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each watch event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who watched the movie */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie watched */,
  "watch_date" TEXT /* Date the movie was watched */,
  "watch_time" TEXT /* Time the movie was watched */,
  "watch_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the watch event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("watch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_watch_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "favorites" (
  "favorite_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each favorite movie */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who favorited the movie */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie favorited */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("favorite_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_favorites_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie reviewed */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was written */,
  "review_time" TEXT /* Time the review was written */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "review_rating" INTEGER /* Rating given in the review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-genre pair */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_categories" (
  "movie_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-category pair */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_categories_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actor_awards" (
  "actor_award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each award */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor who won the award */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  "award_year" INTEGER /* Year the award was won */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_award_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actor_awards_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id")
)
column_names: ID of the movie the actor is in
ID of the movie the director directed
ID of the movie being rated
Source of the rating (e.g., IMDB, Rotten Tomatoes)
Role of the user (e.g., admin, user)
Unique identifier for each watch event
ID of the user who watched the movie
Unique identifier for each favorite movie
ID of the user who favorited the movie
ID of the user who wrote the review
Unique identifier for each movie-genre pair
Unique identifier for each movie-category pair
